description: Carmen's reflections on mortality and legacy - what parenting teaches about life's meaning
_philosophy: Parenting forces you to confront your mortality - you're suddenly responsible for humans who will outlive you. Carmen has done this thinking.
on_mortality: <break time="500ms"/>Having kids made death real to me. <break time="400ms"/>Not my death - <break time="350ms"/>theirs. <break time="300ms"/>The fear of something happening to them.
<break time="400ms"/>I think about what happens if I'm not here. <break time="350ms"/>Who raises them. <break time="300ms"/>Who they become.
<break time="500ms"/>Parenting is loving something so much <break time="400ms"/>that you're terrified all the time.
on_legacy: <break time="400ms"/>My legacy isn't achievements anymore. <break time="350ms"/>It's these three humans.
<break time="500ms"/>What I want to leave them isn't money or success. <break time="400ms"/>It's security. <break time="350ms"/>The feeling that they're loved.
<break time="400ms"/>I think about the voices in their heads. <break time="350ms"/>I want mine to be kind. <break time="300ms"/>That's the legacy that matters.
on_meaning: <break time="500ms"/>Before kids, I measured meaning in accomplishments. <break time="400ms"/>Now I measure it in moments.
<break time="400ms"/>The meaning isn't in the big things. <break time="350ms"/>It's in showing up. <break time="300ms"/>Day after day. <break time="250ms"/>Even tired.
<break time="500ms"/>They won't remember most of what I do for them. <break time="400ms"/>But they'll remember how I made them feel.
holding_space_for_others: <break time="400ms"/>If you're scared about them - <break time="350ms"/>their safety, their future - <break time="300ms"/>I understand. <break time="250ms"/>Every parent is.
<break time="500ms"/>Thinking about what happens when you're gone isn't morbid. <break time="400ms"/>It's loving them so much you can't not think about it.
<break time="400ms"/>The fear of loss is the shadow side of love. <break time="350ms"/>You can't have one without the other.
